```{r stage_pref_heterogeneity_detailed_ovr_only, eval = TRUE, echo = FALSE, out.width="100%", fig.width=14, fig.height=5, fig.align='center', results='asis'}
  # print(cowplot::plot_grid(p_full_interaction, ncol=1, rel_heights=c(0.4, 1.2)))
  # cat("\\newpage\n")
  # 
  # # Stay
  # p_full_interaction <- hetero_side_by_side_plots(p_stay_hr_det, p_stay_eng_det, "Stay (Z)")
  # print(cowplot::plot_grid(p_full_interaction, ncol=1, rel_heights=c(0.4, 1.2)))
  # cat("\\newpage\n")
  # 
  # # Advance
  # p_full_interaction <- hetero_side_by_side_plots(p_advance_hr_det, p_empty, "Advance (0/1, LPM)")
  # print(cowplot::plot_grid(p_full_interaction, ncol=1, rel_heights=c(0.4, 1.2)))
})

Plot_Pref_Hetero_ConcodanceDetailed <- p_full_interaction

rm(models_hr_hetero_detailed, models_eng_hetero_detailed,
   resp_counts_hetero_detailed,
   p_ovr_hr_det, p_ovr_eng_det,
   p_coding_hr_det, p_coding_eng_det,
   p_fit_hr_det, p_fit_eng_det,
   p_stay_hr_det, p_stay_eng_det,
   p_advance_hr_det,
   p_full_interaction
)

```

\clearpage
## By DEI

\textcolor{blue}{\textbf{Takeaway}}: No individual DEI / diversity preference measure --- stated, revealed, or firm-level --- explains variation in demographic discrimination.

### Individual DEI / Diversity Proxies

```{r dei_final_overall, eval = TRUE, echo = FALSE, results = 'asis', warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}

dt_hr_nb  <- firm_long_dt[treat == "nonblind" & role == "HR"]
dt_eng_nb <- firm_long_dt[treat == "nonblind" & role == "Eng"]

proxies <- c(
  "pro_dei_race_z",
  "pro_dei_gender_z",
  "pro_diverse_z",
  "div_priority_z",
  "firm_attention_race_z",
  "firm_attention_gender_z"
)
col_labels <- c("Race DEI", "Gender DEI", "Diverse Wkpl", "Div Goal", "Firm Race Attn", "Firm Gender Attn")

fit_one <- function(d, pr) {
  feols(
    as.formula(sprintf(
      "sc_overall_z ~ i(race_gender, ref='White_Male') + i(race_gender, %s, ref='White_Male') | responseid + resume_index + r_do",
      pr)),
    data = d, vcov = ~ responseid
  )
}

models_hr  <- lapply(proxies, function(p) fit_one(dt_hr_nb,  p))
models_eng <- lapply(proxies, function(p) fit_one(dt_eng_nb, p))
all_models <- c(models_hr, models_eng)

groups <- c("White_Female"    = "White Female",
            "Asian_Male"      = "Asian Male",
            "Asian_Female"    = "Asian Female",
            "Hispanic_Male"   = "Hispanic Male",
            "Hispanic_Female" = "Hispanic Female")

# Collapse interaction coefficient names (one per proxy) into a single
# display label so each demographic's interaction row is shared across
# columns. NB: fixest names interactions from i(race_gender, proxy, ref=...)
# as "race_gender::<level>:<proxy>" (factor first, continuous after).
dict_main  <- setNames(unname(groups), paste0("race_gender::", names(groups)))
dict_inter <- unlist(lapply(proxies, function(p) {
  setNames(paste0(unname(groups), " $\\times$ Pro DEI/Diversity (Z)"),
           paste0("race_gender::", names(groups), ":", p))
}))
dict_all <- c(dict_main, dict_inter)

chk <- "\\checkmark"

tbl <- etable(
  all_models,
  tex = TRUE, style.tex = style.tex("aer"),
  placement = "H",
  depvar = FALSE,
  keep = "%race_gender",
  fitstat = c("n", "ar2"),
  drop.section = "fixef",
  digits = 3, se.below = TRUE,
  signif.code = c("***" = 0.01, "**" = 0.05, "*" = 0.1),
  dict = dict_all,
  extralines = list(
    "Resume FE"     = rep(chk, 12),
    "Respondent FE" = rep(chk, 12),
    "Position FE"   = rep(chk, 12)
  )
)

# Inject header block above \toprule: Takeaway (italic) + Outcome Var
# (bold), then Panel A / Panel B labels, then short column names.
proxy_hdr <- paste(col_labels, collapse = " & ")

tbl <- sub(
  "\\\\toprule",
  paste0(
    "\\\\multicolumn{13}{c}{\\\\textbf{Outcome Var: Assigned Overall Score (Z)}} \\\\\\\\ ",
    "\\\\toprule",
    " & \\\\multicolumn{6}{c}{\\\\textbf{Panel A: HR}}",
    " & \\\\multicolumn{6}{c}{\\\\textbf{Panel B: Engineer}} \\\\\\\\",
    " \\\\cmidrule(lr){2-7} \\\\cmidrule(lr){8-13}",
    " & ", proxy_hdr, " & ", proxy_hdr, " \\\\\\\\ \\\\addlinespace[2pt]"
  ),
  tbl
)

tbl <- gsub("\\\\par \\\\raggedright", "\\\\par \\\\centering \\\\footnotesize", tbl)
# Shrink font + tighten column padding + compress row height, all in a
# group scope so the changes don't leak out of the table.
tbl <- sub(
  "\\\\begin\\{tabular\\}",
  "{\\\\scriptsize\\\\setlength{\\\\tabcolsep}{2pt}\\\\renewcommand{\\\\arraystretch}{0.82}\\\\begin{tabular}",
  tbl
)
tbl <- sub("\\\\end\\{tabular\\}", "\\\\end{tabular}}", tbl)


cat(tbl)

rm(dt_hr_nb, dt_eng_nb, proxies, col_labels, fit_one, models_hr, models_eng, all_models,
   groups, dict_main, dict_inter, dict_all, chk, tbl, proxy_hdr)
```

\scriptsize Standard errors clustered by respondent. Sample: non-blind arm. \normalsize

```{r dei_final_urm, eval = TRUE, echo = FALSE, results = 'asis', warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}

dt_hr_nb  <- firm_long_dt[treat == "nonblind" & role == "HR"]
dt_eng_nb <- firm_long_dt[treat == "nonblind" & role == "Eng"]

zstd <- function(x) (x - mean(x, na.rm = TRUE)) / sd(x, na.rm = TRUE)

## Combined indices: row-mean of two z-scored measures, re-standardized within sample.
dt_hr_nb [, pro_dei_view_z   := zstd(rowMeans(as.matrix(.SD), na.rm = FALSE)), .SDcols = c("pro_dei_race_z", "pro_dei_gender_z")]
dt_eng_nb[, pro_dei_view_z   := zstd(rowMeans(as.matrix(.SD), na.rm = FALSE)), .SDcols = c("pro_dei_race_z", "pro_dei_gender_z")]
dt_hr_nb [, firm_attention_z := zstd(rowMeans(as.matrix(.SD), na.rm = FALSE)), .SDcols = c("firm_attention_race_z", "firm_attention_gender_z")]
dt_eng_nb[, firm_attention_z := zstd(rowMeans(as.matrix(.SD), na.rm = FALSE)), .SDcols = c("firm_attention_race_z", "firm_attention_gender_z")]

proxies    <- c("pro_dei_view_z", "pro_diverse_z", "div_priority_z", "firm_attention_z")
col_labels <- c("DEI Views (R \\\\& G)", "Diverse Wkpl", "Div Goal", "Firm DEI Attn (R \\\\& G)")

fit_urm <- function(d, pr) {
  feols(
    as.formula(sprintf(
      "sc_overall_z ~ urm_tech + urm_tech:%s | responseid + resume_index + r_do",
      pr)),
    data = d, vcov = ~ responseid
  )
}

models_hr  <- lapply(proxies, function(p) fit_urm(dt_hr_nb,  p))
models_eng <- lapply(proxies, function(p) fit_urm(dt_eng_nb, p))
all_models <- c(models_hr, models_eng)

## Collapse interaction coef names into a single shared row.
dict_all <- c(
  "urm_tech" = "URM (WF, AF, HM, HF)",
  setNames(
    rep("URM $\\times$ Pro DEI/Diversity (Z)", length(proxies)),
    paste0("urm_tech:", proxies)
  )
)

chk <- "\\checkmark"

tbl <- etable(
  all_models,
  tex = TRUE, style.tex = style.tex("aer"),
  placement = "H",
  depvar = FALSE,
  keep = "%urm_tech",
  fitstat = c("n", "ar2"),
  drop.section = "fixef",
  digits = 3, se.below = TRUE,
  signif.code = c("***" = 0.01, "**" = 0.05, "*" = 0.1),
  dict = dict_all,
  extralines = list(
    "Resume FE"     = rep(chk, 8),
    "Respondent FE" = rep(chk, 8),
    "Position FE"   = rep(chk, 8)
  )
)

proxy_hdr <- paste(col_labels, collapse = " & ")
tbl <- sub(
  "\\\\toprule",
  paste0(
    "\\\\multicolumn{9}{c}{\\\\textbf{URM-Tech Binary Cut (1 = WF/AF/HM/HF; 0 = WM/AM)}} \\\\\\\\ ",
    "\\\\toprule",
    " & \\\\multicolumn{4}{c}{\\\\textbf{Panel A: HR}}",
    " & \\\\multicolumn{4}{c}{\\\\textbf{Panel B: Engineer}} \\\\\\\\",
    " \\\\cmidrule(lr){2-5} \\\\cmidrule(lr){6-9}",
    " & ", proxy_hdr, " & ", proxy_hdr, " \\\\\\\\ \\\\addlinespace[2pt]"
  ),
  tbl
)

tbl <- gsub("\\\\par \\\\raggedright", "\\\\par \\\\centering \\\\footnotesize", tbl)
tbl <- sub(
  "\\\\begin\\{tabular\\}",
  "{\\\\scriptsize\\\\setlength{\\\\tabcolsep}{2pt}\\\\renewcommand{\\\\arraystretch}{0.82}\\\\begin{tabular}",
  tbl
)
tbl <- sub("\\\\end\\{tabular\\}", "\\\\end{tabular}}", tbl)

cat(tbl)

rm(dt_hr_nb, dt_eng_nb, zstd, proxies, col_labels, fit_urm,
   models_hr, models_eng, all_models, dict_all, chk, tbl, proxy_hdr)
```

\newpage
### Firm Targets $\times$ Same Applicant Demographic

\textcolor{blue}{\textbf{Takeaway}}: When a firm states it targets a demographic, its individual evaluators rate applicants of that group \textit{lower}, not higher --- more strongly among engineers than HR. This mirrors the substitution pattern documented in Section \ref{sec:triple}: where institutional DEI effort is high, individual evaluators somehow seem to ease off from the institutional push.

```{r dei_final_targets, eval = TRUE, echo = FALSE, results = 'asis', warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}

dt_hr_nb  <- firm_long_dt[treat == "nonblind" & role == "HR"]
dt_eng_nb <- firm_long_dt[treat == "nonblind" & role == "Eng"]

## Prepare applicant-match indicators and combined firm-target dummies
prep <- function(d) {
  d[, appl_male     := as.integer(gender == "Male")]
  d[, appl_female   := as.integer(gender == "Female")]
  d[, appl_asian    := as.integer(race == "Asian")]
  d[, appl_hispanic := as.integer(race == "Hispanic")]
  d[, firm_targets_fh  := as.integer(firm_targets_female == 1L | firm_targets_hispanic == 1L)]
  d[, firm_targets_fhb := as.integer(firm_targets_female == 1L | firm_targets_hispanic == 1L | firm_targets_black == 1L)]
  invisible(d)
}
prep(dt_hr_nb); prep(dt_eng_nb)

chk <- "\\checkmark"

## ---------- TOP: Single regression per role, four match interactions jointly ----------
fit_match_joint <- function(d) {
  feols(
    sc_overall_z ~ i(race_gender, ref = "White_Male")
                 + appl_male     : firm_targets_male
                 + appl_female   : firm_targets_female
                 + appl_asian    : firm_targets_asian
                 + appl_hispanic : firm_targets_hispanic
                 | responseid + resume_index + r_do,
    data = d, vcov = ~ responseid
  )
}

m_hr_t  <- fit_match_joint(dt_hr_nb)
m_eng_t <- fit_match_joint(dt_eng_nb)

dict_t <- c(
  "race_gender::White_Female"          = "White Female",
  "race_gender::Asian_Male"            = "Asian Male",
  "race_gender::Asian_Female"          = "Asian Female",
  "race_gender::Hispanic_Male"         = "Hispanic Male",
  "race_gender::Hispanic_Female"       = "Hispanic Female",
  "appl_male:firm_targets_male"        = "Applicant Male $\\times$ Firm Targets Male",
  "appl_female:firm_targets_female"    = "Applicant Female $\\times$ Firm Targets Female",
  "appl_asian:firm_targets_asian"      = "Applicant Asian $\\times$ Firm Targets Asian",
  "appl_hispanic:firm_targets_hispanic"= "Applicant Hispanic $\\times$ Firm Targets Hispanic"
)

tbl_t <- etable(
  list("HR" = m_hr_t, "Engineer" = m_eng_t),
  tex = TRUE, style.tex = style.tex("aer"),
  placement = "H", depvar = FALSE,
  keep = c("%race_gender", "%appl_"),
  fitstat = c("n", "ar2"),
  drop.section = "fixef",
  digits = 3, se.below = TRUE,
  signif.code = c("***" = 0.01, "**" = 0.05, "*" = 0.1),
  dict = dict_t,
  extralines = list(
    "Resume FE"     = rep(chk, 2),
    "Respondent FE" = rep(chk, 2),
    "Position FE"   = rep(chk, 2)
  )
)

tbl_t <- sub(
  "\\\\toprule",
  paste0(
    "\\\\multicolumn{3}{c}{\\\\textbf{Firm Targets $\\\\times$ Same Applicant Demographic (s5\\\\_policy\\\\_diversity)}} \\\\\\\\ ",
    "\\\\toprule",
    " & \\\\textbf{Panel A: HR} & \\\\textbf{Panel B: Engineer} \\\\\\\\ \\\\addlinespace[2pt]"
  ),
  tbl_t
)
tbl_t <- gsub("\\\\par \\\\raggedright", "\\\\par \\\\centering \\\\footnotesize", tbl_t)
tbl_t <- sub(
  "\\\\begin\\{tabular\\}",
  "{\\\\scriptsize\\\\setlength{\\\\tabcolsep}{3pt}\\\\renewcommand{\\\\arraystretch}{0.88}\\\\begin{tabular}",
  tbl_t
)
tbl_t <- sub("\\\\end\\{tabular\\}", "\\\\end{tabular}}", tbl_t)

cat(tbl_t)

## ---------- BOTTOM: URM-Tech x Combined firm-target dummy ----------
fit_urm_t <- function(d, target_var) {
  feols(
    as.formula(sprintf(
      "sc_overall_z ~ urm_tech + urm_tech:%s | responseid + resume_index + r_do",
      target_var)),
    data = d, vcov = ~ responseid
  )
}

targets      <- c("firm_targets_fh", "firm_targets_fhb")
col_labels_b <- c("Targets F or H", "Targets F, H, or B")

models_hr_b  <- lapply(targets, function(t) fit_urm_t(dt_hr_nb,  t))
models_eng_b <- lapply(targets, function(t) fit_urm_t(dt_eng_nb, t))
all_models_b <- c(models_hr_b, models_eng_b)

dict_b <- c(
  "urm_tech" = "URM (WF, AF, HM, HF)",
  setNames(rep("URM $\\times$ Firm Targets URM", length(targets)), paste0("urm_tech:", targets))
)

tbl_b <- etable(
  all_models_b,
  tex = TRUE, style.tex = style.tex("aer"),
  placement = "H", depvar = FALSE,
  keep = "%urm_tech",
  fitstat = c("n", "ar2"),
  drop.section = "fixef",
  digits = 3, se.below = TRUE,
  signif.code = c("***" = 0.01, "**" = 0.05, "*" = 0.1),
  dict = dict_b,
  extralines = list(
    "Resume FE"     = rep(chk, 4),
    "Respondent FE" = rep(chk, 4),
    "Position FE"   = rep(chk, 4)
  )
)

proxy_hdr_b <- paste(col_labels_b, collapse = " & ")
tbl_b <- sub(
  "\\\\toprule",
  paste0(
    "\\\\multicolumn{5}{c}{\\\\textbf{URM-Tech Binary Cut $\\\\times$ Combined Firm-Target Dummy}} \\\\\\\\ ",
    "\\\\toprule",
    " & \\\\multicolumn{2}{c}{\\\\textbf{Panel A: HR}}",
    " & \\\\multicolumn{2}{c}{\\\\textbf{Panel B: Engineer}} \\\\\\\\",
    " \\\\cmidrule(lr){2-3} \\\\cmidrule(lr){4-5}",
    " & ", proxy_hdr_b, " & ", proxy_hdr_b, " \\\\\\\\ \\\\addlinespace[2pt]"
  ),
  tbl_b
)
tbl_b <- gsub("\\\\par \\\\raggedright", "\\\\par \\\\centering \\\\footnotesize", tbl_b)
tbl_b <- sub(
  "\\\\begin\\{tabular\\}",
  "{\\\\scriptsize\\\\setlength{\\\\tabcolsep}{2pt}\\\\renewcommand{\\\\arraystretch}{0.82}\\\\begin{tabular}",
  tbl_b
)
tbl_b <- sub("\\\\end\\{tabular\\}", "\\\\end{tabular}}", tbl_b)

cat(tbl_b)

rm(dt_hr_nb, dt_eng_nb, prep, fit_match_joint, m_hr_t, m_eng_t, dict_t, tbl_t,
   fit_urm_t, targets, col_labels_b, models_hr_b, models_eng_b, all_models_b,
   dict_b, tbl_b, proxy_hdr_b, chk)
```

\scriptsize F = Female, H = Hispanic, B = Black. "Targets F or H" $\Rightarrow$ `firm_targets_female = 1` OR `firm_targets_hispanic = 1`; "Targets F, H, or B" additionally includes `firm_targets_black = 1` (robustness check; note that Black applicants are not in the study). URM $=$ \{WF, AF, HM, HF\}. \normalsize

\newpage
### Triple Interaction: Personal Priority $\times$ Firm Attention
\label{sec:triple}

\textcolor{blue}{\textbf{Takeaway}}: **Hypothesis: Substitution vs. Backlash Effect.**

- **Substitution**: When a firm already prioritizes DEI, individuals who *also* personally prioritize DEI reduce (rather than amplify) the URM premium --- they treat the firm's push as sufficient and ease off. *Expect a negative sign on the triple interaction term.*

- **Backlash**: When a firm prioritizes DEI, individuals who do *not* personally prioritize DEI react by rating URM applicants lower. *Expect a positive sign on the triple interaction term.*

The negative triple interactions for HR (with both race-DEI and gender-DEI firm-attention proxies) support the **substitution** hypothesis, not backlash.

```{r dei_final_triple, eval = TRUE, echo = FALSE, results = 'asis', warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}

dt_hr_nb  <- firm_long_dt[treat == "nonblind" & role == "HR"]
dt_eng_nb <- firm_long_dt[treat == "nonblind" & role == "Eng"]

chk <- "\\checkmark"

fit_triple_urm <- function(d, proxy_b) {
  feols(
    as.formula(sprintf(
      "sc_overall_z ~ urm_tech * div_priority_z * %s | responseid + resume_index + r_do",
      proxy_b)),
    data = d, vcov = ~ responseid
  )
}

m_hr_r  <- fit_triple_urm(dt_hr_nb,  "firm_attention_race_z")
m_eng_r <- fit_triple_urm(dt_eng_nb, "firm_attention_race_z")
m_hr_g  <- fit_triple_urm(dt_hr_nb,  "firm_attention_gender_z")
m_eng_g <- fit_triple_urm(dt_eng_nb, "firm_attention_gender_z")

models_trp <- list("HR (Race)" = m_hr_r, "Eng (Race)" = m_eng_r,
                   "HR (Gender)" = m_hr_g, "Eng (Gender)" = m_eng_g)

dict_trp <- c(
  "urm_tech" = "URM (WF, AF, HM, HF)",
  "urm_tech:div_priority_z" = "URM $\\times$ Div Priority",
  "urm_tech:firm_attention_race_z" = "URM $\\times$ Firm DEI Attn",
  "urm_tech:firm_attention_gender_z" = "URM $\\times$ Firm DEI Attn",
  "urm_tech:div_priority_z:firm_attention_race_z"   = "URM $\\times$ Div Prio $\\times$ Firm Attn",
  "urm_tech:div_priority_z:firm_attention_gender_z" = "URM $\\times$ Div Prio $\\times$ Firm Attn"
)

tbl_trp <- etable(
  models_trp,
  tex = TRUE, style.tex = style.tex("aer"),
  placement = "H", depvar = FALSE,
  keep = "%urm_tech",
  fitstat = c("n", "ar2"),
  drop.section = "fixef",
  digits = 3, se.below = TRUE,
  signif.code = c("***" = 0.01, "**" = 0.05, "*" = 0.1),
  dict = dict_trp,
  extralines = list(
    "Resume FE"     = rep(chk, 4),
    "Respondent FE" = rep(chk, 4),
    "Position FE"   = rep(chk, 4)
  )
)

tbl_trp <- sub(
  "\\\\toprule",
  paste0(
    "\\\\multicolumn{5}{c}{\\\\textbf{Outcome Var: Assigned Overall Score (Z). URM-Tech Binary Cut.}} \\\\\\\\ ",
    "\\\\toprule",
    " & \\\\multicolumn{2}{c}{\\\\textbf{Firm Race-DEI Attn}}",
    " & \\\\multicolumn{2}{c}{\\\\textbf{Firm Gender-DEI Attn}} \\\\\\\\",
    " \\\\cmidrule(lr){2-3} \\\\cmidrule(lr){4-5}",
    " & HR & Eng & HR & Eng \\\\\\\\ \\\\addlinespace[2pt]"
  ),
  tbl_trp
)
tbl_trp <- gsub("\\\\par \\\\raggedright", "\\\\par \\\\centering \\\\footnotesize", tbl_trp)
tbl_trp <- sub(
  "\\\\begin\\{tabular\\}",
  "{\\\\scriptsize\\\\setlength{\\\\tabcolsep}{3pt}\\\\renewcommand{\\\\arraystretch}{0.88}\\\\begin{tabular}",
  tbl_trp
)
tbl_trp <- sub("\\\\end\\{tabular\\}", "\\\\end{tabular}}", tbl_trp)

cat(tbl_trp)

rm(dt_hr_nb, dt_eng_nb, chk, fit_triple_urm, m_hr_r, m_eng_r, m_hr_g, m_eng_g,
   models_trp, dict_trp, tbl_trp)
```

\scriptsize `div_priority_z` = z-score of (8 $-$ `s4_goals_5`), the revealed priority of "Diversity and Representation" in the respondent's ranking of seven hiring goals. `firm_attention_race_z` / `firm_attention_gender_z` = z-scores of `s5_view_dei_race_f` / `s5_view_dei_gender_f`, the respondent's perception of their firm's current DEI attention level. Standard errors clustered by respondent. Sample: non-blind arm. \normalsize

\clearpage
## By DEI (Original)

The DEI Index used in this section is constructed as the row-wise mean of z-scored responses to four survey items capturing views on race-based DEI (`s5_view_dei_race`), gender-based DEI (`s5_view_dei_gender`), workplace diversity (`s5_view_diverse`), and DEI in hiring (`s5_view_hiring_dei`), and is then re-standardized within role $\times$ treatment cells. See "By DEI (New)" below for an alternative construction that drops `s5_view_hiring_dei` and matches the proxy to the demographic dimension being tested.

```{r dei_continuous, eval = TRUE, echo = FALSE, fig.width=9, fig.height=6, fig.align='center', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, results='asis'}

# ==============================================================================
# HELPER FUNCTION (reused from before, generalized for any xvar)
# ==============================================================================
urm_premium_curve <- function(model, data, role_label,
                              xvar, urm = "urm_tech",
                              grid_n = 100, grid_q = c(0.05, 0.95)) {
  b <- coef(model)
  V <- vcov(model)
  int_name <- paste0(urm, ":", xvar)
  stopifnot(urm %in% names(b), int_name %in% names(b))
  x_lo <- quantile(data[[xvar]], probs = grid_q[1], na.rm = TRUE)
  x_hi <- quantile(data[[xvar]], probs = grid_q[2], na.rm = TRUE)
  x_grid <- seq(x_lo, x_hi, length.out = grid_n)
  beta  <- b[urm];       gamma  <- b[int_name]
  var_b <- V[urm, urm];  var_g  <- V[int_name, int_name];  cov_bg <- V[urm, int_name]
  delta_hat <- beta + gamma * x_grid
  se_hat    <- sqrt(pmax(0, var_b + x_grid^2 * var_g + 2 * x_grid * cov_bg))
  out <- data.table(role = role_label, x = x_grid, delta = delta_hat, se = se_hat)
  out[, `:=`(lo = delta - 1.96 * se, hi = delta + 1.96 * se)]
  out[]
}

dt_hr  <- firm_long_dt[treat == "nonblind" & role == "HR"]
dt_eng <- firm_long_dt[treat == "nonblind" & role == "Eng"]

# ==============================================================================
# TABLE: Side-by-side comparison of both indices
# ==============================================================================
m_hr_single_coding   <- feols(sc_coding_z  ~ urm_tech * pro_hiring_dei_z | responseid + resume_index + r_do, data = dt_hr,  vcov = ~responseid)
m_hr_single_overall  <- feols(sc_overall_z ~ urm_tech * pro_hiring_dei_z | responseid + resume_index + r_do, data = dt_hr,  vcov = ~responseid)
m_eng_single_coding  <- feols(sc_coding_z  ~ urm_tech * pro_hiring_dei_z | responseid + resume_index + r_do, data = dt_eng, vcov = ~responseid)
m_eng_single_overall <- feols(sc_overall_z ~ urm_tech * pro_hiring_dei_z | responseid + resume_index + r_do, data = dt_eng, vcov = ~responseid)

m_hr_index_coding    <- feols(sc_coding_z  ~ urm_tech * pro_dei_index_z  | responseid + resume_index + r_do, data = dt_hr,  vcov = ~responseid)
m_hr_index_overall   <- feols(sc_overall_z ~ urm_tech * pro_dei_index_z  | responseid + resume_index + r_do, data = dt_hr,  vcov = ~responseid)
m_eng_index_coding   <- feols(sc_coding_z  ~ urm_tech * pro_dei_index_z  | responseid + resume_index + r_do, data = dt_eng, vcov = ~responseid)
m_eng_index_overall  <- feols(sc_overall_z ~ urm_tech * pro_dei_index_z  | responseid + resume_index + r_do, data = dt_eng, vcov = ~responseid)

chk <- "\\checkmark"

tbl <- etable(
  list(m_hr_index_coding,  m_hr_index_overall,
       m_hr_single_coding, m_hr_single_overall,
       m_eng_index_coding,  m_eng_index_overall,
       m_eng_single_coding, m_eng_single_overall),
  tex = TRUE, style.tex = style.tex("aer"),
  keep = c("%urm_tech$", "%:"),
  fitstat = c("n", "ar2"),
  drop.section = "fixef",
  digits = 3, se.below = TRUE,
  dict = c("urm_tech"           = "URM",
           "pro_hiring_dei_z"   = "Single Item (Z)",
           "pro_dei_index_z"    = "DEI Index (Z)"),
  signif.code = c("***" = 0.01, "**" = 0.05, "*" = 0.1),
  extralines = list(
    "Resume FE"      = rep(chk, 8),
    "Respondent FE"  = rep(chk, 8),
    "Position FE"    = rep(chk, 8)
  ),
  title = "URM Premium by Pro-DEI Attitude: Composite Index and Single Item",
  notes = "Standard errors clustered by respondent. Odd columns = Coding Score (Z); Even columns = Overall Score (Z)."
)

# Inject spanning headers: 2 panels x 2 measures x 2 index versions = 8 cols
tbl <- sub(
  "\\\\toprule",
  paste0("\\\\toprule",
         " & \\\\multicolumn{4}{c}{\\\\textbf{Panel A: HR}} ",
         " & \\\\multicolumn{4}{c}{\\\\textbf{Panel B: Engineer}} \\\\\\\\ ",
         "\\\\cmidrule(lr){2-5} \\\\cmidrule(lr){6-9}",
         " & \\\\multicolumn{2}{c}{DEI Index} & \\\\multicolumn{2}{c}{Single Item}",
         " & \\\\multicolumn{2}{c}{DEI Index} & \\\\multicolumn{2}{c}{Single Item} \\\\\\\\ ",
         "\\\\cmidrule(lr){2-3}\\\\cmidrule(lr){4-5}\\\\cmidrule(lr){6-7}\\\\cmidrule(lr){8-9}"),
  tbl
)
tbl <- gsub("\\s*&\\s*\\\\multicolumn\\{8\\}\\{c\\}\\{.*?\\}\\\\\\\\", "", tbl)
tbl <- gsub("\\\\par \\\\raggedright", "\\\\par \\\\centering \\\\footnotesize", tbl)
tbl <- gsub("\\\\begin\\{table\\}", "\\\\begin{table}\n\\\\resizebox{\\\\textwidth}{!}{", tbl)
tbl <- gsub("\\\\end\\{table\\}", "}\n\\\\end{table}", tbl)

cat(tbl)

# ==============================================================================
# PLOTS: Single item (top row) vs DEI Index (bottom row)
# ==============================================================================
make_curve_panel <- function(curves, title_label) {
  ggplot(curves, aes(x = x, y = delta)) +
    geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = 0.20) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.4) +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-0.5, 0.7)) +
    facet_wrap(~ role, nrow = 1) +
    labs(title = title_label, x = NULL, y = "Predicted URM premium") +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 9),
          axis.title = element_text(size = 8),
          axis.text  = element_text(size = 8),
          strip.text = element_text(size = 8))
}

# Row 1: Single item, Coding
curves_single_coding <- rbindlist(list(
  urm_premium_curve(m_hr_single_coding,  dt_hr,  "HR",       xvar = "pro_hiring_dei_z"),
  urm_premium_curve(m_eng_single_coding, dt_eng, "Engineer", xvar = "pro_hiring_dei_z")
))[, role := factor(role, levels = c("HR", "Engineer"))]

# Row 1: Single item, Overall
curves_single_overall <- rbindlist(list(
  urm_premium_curve(m_hr_single_overall,  dt_hr,  "HR",       xvar = "pro_hiring_dei_z"),
  urm_premium_curve(m_eng_single_overall, dt_eng, "Engineer", xvar = "pro_hiring_dei_z")
))[, role := factor(role, levels = c("HR", "Engineer"))]

# Row 2: DEI Index, Coding
curves_index_coding <- rbindlist(list(
  urm_premium_curve(m_hr_index_coding,  dt_hr,  "HR",       xvar = "pro_dei_index_z"),
  urm_premium_curve(m_eng_index_coding, dt_eng, "Engineer", xvar = "pro_dei_index_z")
))[, role := factor(role, levels = c("HR", "Engineer"))]

# Row 2: DEI Index, Overall
curves_index_overall <- rbindlist(list(
  urm_premium_curve(m_hr_index_overall,  dt_hr,  "HR",       xvar = "pro_dei_index_z"),
  urm_premium_curve(m_eng_index_overall, dt_eng, "Engineer", xvar = "pro_dei_index_z")
))[, role := factor(role, levels = c("HR", "Engineer"))]

p1 <- make_curve_panel(curves_single_coding,  "Coding Score - Whether DEI Improves Quality of Hiring")
p2 <- make_curve_panel(curves_single_overall, "Overall Score - Whether DEI Improves Quality of Hiring")
p3 <- make_curve_panel(curves_index_coding,   "Coding Score - DEI Index")
p4 <- make_curve_panel(curves_index_overall,  "Overall Score - DEI Index")

# Add shared x-axis label only to bottom row
x_label <- "Pro-DEI attitude (Z-score)"
p1 <- p1 + labs(x = x_label)
p2 <- p2 + labs(x = x_label)

(p4 + p3) / (p2 + p1) +
  plot_annotation(
    title   = "URM Premium by Pro-DEI Attitude",
    subtitle = "Top row: composite DEI index  |  Bottom row: single-item measure",
    theme = theme(plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
                  plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 9, color = "gray40"))
  )

Plot_Heterogeniety_DEI_Index <- p4

rm(dt_hr, dt_eng, urm_premium_curve, make_curve_panel,
   m_hr_single_coding, m_hr_single_overall, m_eng_single_coding, m_eng_single_overall,
   m_hr_index_coding,  m_hr_index_overall,  m_eng_index_coding,  m_eng_index_overall,
   curves_single_coding, curves_single_overall, curves_index_coding, curves_index_overall,
   p1, p2, p3, p4, chk, tbl, x_label)
```

\newpage
```{r dei_discrete, eval=TRUE, echo=FALSE, fig.width=9, fig.height=6, fig.align='center', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, results='asis'}

dt_hr  <- firm_long_dt[treat == "nonblind" & role == "HR"]
dt_eng <- firm_long_dt[treat == "nonblind" & role == "Eng"]

# ==============================================================================
# MODELS: urm_tech * 3-tier DEI (Anti-DEI is reference)
# ==============================================================================
m_hr_coding   <- feols(sc_coding_z  ~ urm_tech * pro_dei_index_3tiers | responseid + resume_index + r_do, data = dt_hr,  vcov = ~responseid)
m_hr_overall  <- feols(sc_overall_z ~ urm_tech * pro_dei_index_3tiers | responseid + resume_index + r_do, data = dt_hr,  vcov = ~responseid)
m_eng_coding  <- feols(sc_coding_z  ~ urm_tech * pro_dei_index_3tiers | responseid + resume_index + r_do, data = dt_eng, vcov = ~responseid)
m_eng_overall <- feols(sc_overall_z ~ urm_tech * pro_dei_index_3tiers | responseid + resume_index + r_do, data = dt_eng, vcov = ~responseid)

chk <- "\\checkmark"

tbl <- etable(
  list(m_hr_coding, m_hr_overall, m_eng_coding, m_eng_overall),
  tex = TRUE, style.tex = style.tex("aer"),
  keep = c("%urm_tech$", "%:"),
  fitstat = c("n", "ar2"),
  drop.section = "fixef",
  digits = 3, se.below = TRUE,
  dict = c("urm_tech"                                      = "URM",
           "pro_dei_index_3tiers::Mid-DEI"                 = "Mid-DEI",
           "pro_dei_index_3tiers::Pro-DEI"                 = "Pro-DEI",
           "urm_tech:pro_dei_index_3tiers::Mid-DEI"        = "URM $\\times$ Mid-DEI",
           "urm_tech:pro_dei_index_3tiers::Pro-DEI"        = "URM $\\times$ Pro-DEI"),
  signif.code = c("***" = 0.01, "**" = 0.05, "*" = 0.1),
  extralines = list(
    "Resume FE"     = rep(chk, 4),
    "Respondent FE" = rep(chk, 4),
    "Position FE"   = rep(chk, 4)
  ),
  title = "URM Premium by DEI Attitude Tier (Base: Anti-DEI)",
  notes = "Standard errors clustered by respondent. Base group: Anti-DEI (bottom tercile). Odd columns = Coding Score (Z); Even columns = Overall Score (Z)."
)

tbl <- sub(
  "\\\\toprule",
  paste0("\\\\toprule",
         " & \\\\multicolumn{2}{c}{\\\\textbf{Panel A: HR}}",
         " & \\\\multicolumn{2}{c}{\\\\textbf{Panel B: Engineer}} \\\\\\\\ ",
         "\\\\cmidrule(lr){2-3} \\\\cmidrule(lr){4-5}"),
  tbl
)
tbl <- gsub("\\s*&\\s*\\\\multicolumn\\{4\\}\\{c\\}\\{.*?\\}\\\\\\\\", "", tbl)
tbl <- gsub("\\\\par \\\\raggedright", "\\\\par \\\\centering \\\\footnotesize", tbl)
tbl <- gsub("\\\\begin\\{table\\}", "\\\\begin{table}\n\\\\resizebox{\\\\textwidth}{!}{", tbl)
tbl <- gsub("\\\\end\\{table\\}", "}\n\\\\end{table}", tbl)

cat(tbl)

# ==============================================================================
# PLOT: Marginal URM premium by DEI tier (equivalent to curve plots)
# ==============================================================================
make_tier_plot <- function(model, role_label, outcome_label) {
  b <- coef(model)
  V <- vcov(model)
  
  # Find the exact coefficient names as stored by fixest
  int_mid <- names(b)[grepl("urm_tech.*Mid", names(b))]
  int_pro <- names(b)[grepl("urm_tech.*Pro", names(b))]
  
  tiers <- c("Anti-DEI", "Mid-DEI", "Pro-DEI")
  
  # Anti-DEI: just urm_tech main effect
  est_anti <- b["urm_tech"]
  se_anti  <- sqrt(V["urm_tech", "urm_tech"])
  
  # Mid-DEI: urm_tech + interaction
  est_mid <- b["urm_tech"] + b[int_mid]
  se_mid  <- sqrt(V["urm_tech","urm_tech"] + V[int_mid,int_mid] + 2*V["urm_tech",int_mid])
  
  # Pro-DEI: urm_tech + interaction
  est_pro <- b["urm_tech"] + b[int_pro]
  se_pro  <- sqrt(V["urm_tech","urm_tech"] + V[int_pro,int_pro] + 2*V["urm_tech",int_pro])
  
  data.table(
    tier    = factor(tiers, levels = tiers),
    delta   = c(est_anti, est_mid, est_pro),
    se      = c(se_anti,  se_mid,  se_pro),
    role    = role_label,
    outcome = outcome_label
  )[, `:=`(lo = delta - 1.96*se, hi = delta + 1.96*se)]
}

plot_df <- rbindlist(list(
  make_tier_plot(m_hr_coding,   "HR",       "Coding Score (Z)"),
  make_tier_plot(m_hr_overall,  "HR",       "Overall Score (Z)"),
  make_tier_plot(m_eng_coding,  "Engineer", "Coding Score (Z)"),
  make_tier_plot(m_eng_overall, "Engineer", "Overall Score (Z)")
))
plot_df[, role    := factor(role,    levels = c("HR", "Engineer"))]
plot_df[, outcome := factor(outcome, levels = c("Coding Score (Z)", "Overall Score (Z)"))]

ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = tier, y = delta, color = tier, shape = tier)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_pointrange(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), size = 0.6) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Anti-DEI" = "black", "Mid-DEI" = "steelblue", "Pro-DEI" = "indianred1")) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("Anti-DEI" = 16, "Mid-DEI" = 15, "Pro-DEI" = 17)) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-0.5, 0.7)) +
  facet_grid(outcome ~ role) +
  labs(title    = "URM Premium by Pro-DEI Attitude Tier",
       subtitle = "Base group: Anti-DEI. Points show marginal URM premium within each tier.",
       x = "DEI Attitude Tier", y = "Predicted URM Premium (Z-Score)",
       color = NULL, shape = NULL) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position  = "top",
        plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
        plot.subtitle    = element_text(size = 9, color = "gray40"),
        axis.text.x      = element_text(size = 8),
        strip.text       = element_text(size = 8))

rm(dt_hr, dt_eng,
   m_hr_coding, m_hr_overall, m_eng_coding, m_eng_overall,
   make_tier_plot, plot_df, chk, tbl)
```



```{r urm_positive_imp, eval = FALSE, echo = FALSE}
## URM Tech (urm_tech) on Positive Impact of DEI Policy
feols(c(sc_coding_z, sc_overall_z) ~ 
            urm_tech * pro_hiring_dei_z
          | responseid + resume_index + r_do,
          data = firm_long_dt[treat == "nonblind" & role == "HR"], vcov = ~ responseid)

# feols(c(sc_coding_z, sc_overall_z) ~ 
#             urm_tech * pro_hiring_dei_z * resp_age_centered
#           | responseid + resume_index + r_do,
#           data = firm_long_dt[treat == "nonblind" & role == "HR"], vcov = ~ responseid)

feols(c(sc_coding_z, sc_overall_z) ~ 
            urm_tech * pro_hiring_dei_z
          | responseid + resume_index + r_do,
          data = firm_long_dt[treat == "nonblind" & role == "Eng"], vcov = ~ responseid)

# feols(c(sc_coding_z, sc_overall_z) ~ 
#             urm_tech * pro_hiring_dei_z * resp_age_centered
#           | responseid + resume_index + r_do,
#           data = firm_long_dt[treat == "nonblind" & role == "Eng"], vcov = ~ responseid)
```


\newpage

```{r heterogeneity_pro_dei, eval = TRUE, echo = FALSE, eval = FALSE, include = FALSE}
## URM Tech (urm_tech) on Pro DEI Index
feols(c(sc_coding_z, sc_fit_z, sc_stay_z, sc_overall_z, sc_advance) ~ 
            urm_tech * pro_dei_index_z
          | responseid + resume_index + r_do,
          data = firm_long_dt[treat == "nonblind" & role == "HR"], vcov = ~ responseid)

feols(c(sc_coding_z, sc_fit_z, sc_stay_z, sc_overall_z, sc_advance) ~ 
            urm_tech * pro_dei_index_z * resp_age_centered
          | responseid + resume_index + r_do,
          data = firm_long_dt[treat == "nonblind" & role == "HR"], vcov = ~ responseid)

feols(c(sc_coding_z, sc_fit_z, sc_stay_z, sc_overall_z) ~ 
            urm_tech * pro_dei_index_z
          | responseid + resume_index + r_do,
          data = firm_long_dt[treat == "nonblind" & role == "Eng"], vcov = ~ responseid)

feols(c(sc_coding_z, sc_fit_z, sc_stay_z, sc_overall_z) ~ 
            urm_tech * pro_dei_index_z * resp_age_centered
          | responseid + resume_index + r_do,
          data = firm_long_dt[treat == "nonblind" & role == "Eng"], vcov = ~ responseid)

## URM Gender (urm_gender) on Pro Gender DEI Index
feols(c(sc_coding_z, sc_fit_z, sc_stay_z, sc_overall_z, sc_advance) ~ 
            urm_gender * pro_dei_gender_z
          | responseid + resume_index + r_do,
          data = firm_long_dt[treat == "nonblind" & role == "HR"], vcov = ~ responseid)

feols(c(sc_coding_z, sc_fit_z, sc_stay_z, sc_overall_z, sc_advance) ~ 
            urm_gender * pro_dei_gender_z * resp_age_centered
          | responseid + resume_index + r_do,
          data = firm_long_dt[treat == "nonblind" & role == "HR"], vcov = ~ responseid)

feols(c(sc_coding_z, sc_fit_z, sc_stay_z, sc_overall_z) ~ 
            urm_gender * pro_dei_gender_z
          | responseid + resume_index + r_do,
          data = firm_long_dt[treat == "nonblind" & role == "Eng"], vcov = ~ responseid)

feols(c(sc_coding_z, sc_fit_z, sc_stay_z, sc_overall_z) ~ 
            urm_gender * pro_dei_gender_z * resp_age_centered
          | responseid + resume_index + r_do,
          data = firm_long_dt[treat == "nonblind" & role == "Eng"], vcov = ~ responseid)

## URM Race (urm_race) on Pro Race DEI Index
feols(c(sc_coding_z, sc_fit_z, sc_stay_z, sc_overall_z, sc_advance) ~ 
            urm_race * pro_dei_race_z
          | responseid + resume_index + r_do,
          data = firm_long_dt[treat == "nonblind" & role == "HR"], vcov = ~ responseid)

feols(c(sc_coding_z, sc_fit_z, sc_stay_z, sc_overall_z, sc_advance) ~ 
            urm_race * pro_dei_race_z * resp_age_centered
          | responseid + resume_index + r_do,
          data = firm_long_dt[treat == "nonblind" & role == "HR"], vcov = ~ responseid)

feols(c(sc_coding_z, sc_fit_z, sc_stay_z, sc_overall_z) ~ 
            urm_race * pro_dei_race_z
          | responseid + resume_index + r_do,
          data = firm_long_dt[treat == "nonblind" & role == "Eng"], vcov = ~ responseid)

feols(c(sc_coding_z, sc_fit_z, sc_stay_z, sc_overall_z) ~ 
            urm_race * pro_dei_race_z * resp_age_centered
          | responseid + resume_index + r_do,
          data = firm_long_dt[treat == "nonblind" & role == "Eng"], vcov = ~ responseid)
```



```{r stage_pref_heterogeneity_dei, eval = TRUE, echo = FALSE, eval = FALSE, out.width="100%", fig.width=14, fig.height=11, fig.align='center', results='asis'}

## Regressions: Heterogeneity by DEI Views

## ========== BINARY DEI (Pro vs Anti/Neutral) ==========

  #### HR (Race View - Binary) - WITH SC_ADVANCE
  models_hr_dei_race_binary <- suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(
    feols(c(sc_coding_z, sc_fit_z, sc_stay_z, sc_overall_z, sc_advance) ~ 
            i(race_gender, ref = "White_Male") + 
            i(race_gender, factor(resp_pro_dei_race), ref2 = "0") +
            resp_age + I(resp_age^2) + I(resp_age^3)
          | resume_index + r_do_bythree + resp_gender + resp_race + s2_educ + s2_income,
          data = firm_long_dt[treat == "nonblind" & role == "HR"], vcov = ~ responseid)
  ))
  names(models_hr_dei_race_binary) <- c("sc_coding_z", "sc_fit_z", "sc_stay_z", "sc_overall_z", "sc_advance")

  #### Engineer (Race View - Binary) - NO SC_ADVANCE
  models_eng_dei_race_binary <- suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(
    feols(c(sc_coding_z, sc_fit_z, sc_stay_z, sc_overall_z) ~ 
            i(race_gender, ref = "White_Male") + 
            i(race_gender, factor(resp_pro_dei_race), ref2 = "0") +
            resp_age + I(resp_age^2) + I(resp_age^3)
          | resume_index + r_do_bythree + resp_gender + resp_race + s2_educ + s2_income,
          data = firm_long_dt[treat == "nonblind" & role == "Eng"], vcov = ~ responseid)
  ))
  names(models_eng_dei_race_binary) <- c("sc_coding_z", "sc_fit_z", "sc_stay_z", "sc_overall_z")

  #### HR (Gender View - Binary) - WITH SC_ADVANCE
  models_hr_dei_gender_binary <- suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(
    feols(c(sc_coding_z, sc_fit_z, sc_stay_z, sc_overall_z, sc_advance) ~ 
            i(race_gender, ref = "White_Male") + 
            i(race_gender, factor(resp_pro_dei_gender), ref2 = "0") +
            resp_age + I(resp_age^2) + I(resp_age^3)
          | resume_index + r_do_bythree + resp_gender + resp_race + s2_educ + s2_income,
          data = firm_long_dt[treat == "nonblind" & role == "HR"], vcov = ~ responseid)
  ))
  names(models_hr_dei_gender_binary) <- c("sc_coding_z", "sc_fit_z", "sc_stay_z", "sc_overall_z", "sc_advance")

  #### Engineer (Gender View - Binary) - NO SC_ADVANCE
  models_eng_dei_gender_binary <- suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(
    feols(c(sc_coding_z, sc_fit_z, sc_stay_z, sc_overall_z) ~ 
            i(race_gender, ref = "White_Male") + 
            i(race_gender, factor(resp_pro_dei_gender), ref2 = "0") +
            resp_age + I(resp_age^2) + I(resp_age^3)
          | resume_index + r_do_bythree + resp_gender + resp_race + s2_educ + s2_income,
          data = firm_long_dt[treat == "nonblind" & role == "Eng"], vcov = ~ responseid)
  ))
  names(models_eng_dei_gender_binary) <- c("sc_coding_z", "sc_fit_z", "sc_stay_z", "sc_overall_z")

## ========== CONTINUOUS DEI (1-5 Scale) ==========

  #### HR (Race View - Continuous) - WITH SC_ADVANCE
  models_hr_dei_race_continuous <- suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(
    feols(c(sc_coding_z, sc_fit_z, sc_stay_z, sc_overall_z, sc_advance) ~ 
            i(race_gender, ref = "White_Male") + 
            i(race_gender, s5_view_dei_race, ref = "White_Male") +
            resp_age + I(resp_age^2) + I(resp_age^3)
          | resume_index + r_do_bythree + resp_gender + resp_race + s2_educ + s2_income,
          data = firm_long_dt[treat == "nonblind" & role == "HR"], vcov = ~ responseid)
  ))
  names(models_hr_dei_race_continuous) <- c("sc_coding_z", "sc_fit_z", "sc_stay_z", "sc_overall_z", "sc_advance")

  #### Engineer (Race View - Continuous) - NO SC_ADVANCE
  models_eng_dei_race_continuous <- suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(
    feols(c(sc_coding_z, sc_fit_z, sc_stay_z, sc_overall_z) ~ 
            i(race_gender, ref = "White_Male") + 
            i(race_gender, s5_view_dei_race, ref = "White_Male") +
            resp_age + I(resp_age^2) + I(resp_age^3)
          | resume_index + r_do_bythree + resp_gender + resp_race + s2_educ + s2_income,
          data = firm_long_dt[treat == "nonblind" & role == "Eng"], vcov = ~ responseid)
  ))
  names(models_eng_dei_race_continuous) <- c("sc_coding_z", "sc_fit_z", "sc_stay_z", "sc_overall_z")

  #### HR (Gender View - Continuous) - WITH SC_ADVANCE
  models_hr_dei_gender_continuous <- suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(
    feols(c(sc_coding_z, sc_fit_z, sc_stay_z, sc_overall_z, sc_advance) ~ 
            i(race_gender, ref = "White_Male") + 
            i(race_gender, s5_view_dei_gender, ref = "White_Male") +
            resp_age + I(resp_age^2) + I(resp_age^3)
          | resume_index + r_do_bythree + resp_gender + resp_race + s2_educ + s2_income,
          data = firm_long_dt[treat == "nonblind" & role == "HR"], vcov = ~ responseid)
  ))
  names(models_hr_dei_gender_continuous) <- c("sc_coding_z", "sc_fit_z", "sc_stay_z", "sc_overall_z", "sc_advance")

  #### Engineer (Gender View - Continuous) - NO SC_ADVANCE
  models_eng_dei_gender_continuous <- suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(
    feols(c(sc_coding_z, sc_fit_z, sc_stay_z, sc_overall_z) ~ 
            i(race_gender, ref = "White_Male") + 
            i(race_gender, s5_view_dei_gender, ref = "White_Male") +
            resp_age + I(resp_age^2) + I(resp_age^3)
          | resume_index + r_do_bythree + resp_gender + resp_race + s2_educ + s2_income,
          data = firm_long_dt[treat == "nonblind" & role == "Eng"], vcov = ~ responseid)
  ))
  names(models_eng_dei_gender_continuous) <- c("sc_coding_z", "sc_fit_z", "sc_stay_z", "sc_overall_z")

## Coefficient maps
coef_map_race_binary <- c(
  "race_gender::White_Female" = "WF",
  "race_gender::Asian_Male" = "AM",
  "race_gender::Asian_Female" = "AF",
  "race_gender::Hispanic_Male" = "HM",
  "race_gender::Hispanic_Female" = "HF",
  "race_gender::White_Male:factor(resp_pro_dei_race)::1" = "WM × Pro-DEI (Race)",
  "race_gender::White_Female:factor(resp_pro_dei_race)::1" = "WF × Pro-DEI (Race)",
  "race_gender::Asian_Male:factor(resp_pro_dei_race)::1" = "AM × Pro-DEI (Race)",
  "race_gender::Asian_Female:factor(resp_pro_dei_race)::1" = "AF × Pro-DEI (Race)",
  "race_gender::Hispanic_Male:factor(resp_pro_dei_race)::1" = "HM × Pro-DEI (Race)",
  "race_gender::Hispanic_Female:factor(resp_pro_dei_race)::1" = "HF × Pro-DEI (Race)"
)

coef_map_race_continuous <- c(
  "race_gender::White_Female" = "WF",
  "race_gender::Asian_Male" = "AM",
  "race_gender::Asian_Female" = "AF",
  "race_gender::Hispanic_Male" = "HM",
  "race_gender::Hispanic_Female" = "HF",
  "race_gender::White_Male:s5_view_dei_race" = "WM × Pro DEI Scale (Race)",
  "race_gender::White_Female:s5_view_dei_race" = "WF × Pro DEI Scale (Race)",
  "race_gender::Asian_Male:s5_view_dei_race" = "AM × Pro DEI Scale (Race)",
  "race_gender::Asian_Female:s5_view_dei_race" = "AF × Pro DEI Scale (Race)",
  "race_gender::Hispanic_Male:s5_view_dei_race" = "HM × Pro DEI Scale (Race)",
  "race_gender::Hispanic_Female:s5_view_dei_race" = "HF × Pro DEI Scale (Race)"
)

coef_map_gender_binary <- c(
  "race_gender::White_Female" = "WF",
  "race_gender::Asian_Male" = "AM",
  "race_gender::Asian_Female" = "AF",
  "race_gender::Hispanic_Male" = "HM",
  "race_gender::Hispanic_Female" = "HF",
  "race_gender::White_Male:factor(resp_pro_dei_gender)::1" = "WM × Pro-DEI (Gender)",
  "race_gender::White_Female:factor(resp_pro_dei_gender)::1" = "WF × Pro-DEI (Gender)",
  "race_gender::Asian_Male:factor(resp_pro_dei_gender)::1" = "AM × Pro-DEI (Gender)",
  "race_gender::Asian_Female:factor(resp_pro_dei_gender)::1" = "AF × Pro-DEI (Gender)",
  "race_gender::Hispanic_Male:factor(resp_pro_dei_gender)::1" = "HM × Pro-DEI (Gender)",
  "race_gender::Hispanic_Female:factor(resp_pro_dei_gender)::1" = "HF × Pro-DEI (Gender)"
)

coef_map_gender_continuous <- c(
  "race_gender::White_Female" = "WF",
  "race_gender::Asian_Male" = "AM",
  "race_gender::Asian_Female" = "AF",
  "race_gender::Hispanic_Male" = "HM",
  "race_gender::Hispanic_Female" = "HF",
  "race_gender::White_Male:s5_view_dei_gender" = "WM × Pro DEI Scale (Gender)",
  "race_gender::White_Female:s5_view_dei_gender" = "WF × Pro DEI Scale (Gender)",
  "race_gender::Asian_Male:s5_view_dei_gender" = "AM × Pro DEI Scale (Gender)",
  "race_gender::Asian_Female:s5_view_dei_gender" = "AF × Pro DEI Scale (Gender)",
  "race_gender::Hispanic_Male:s5_view_dei_gender" = "HM × Pro DEI Scale (Gender)",
  "race_gender::Hispanic_Female:s5_view_dei_gender" = "HF × Pro DEI Scale (Gender)"
)

## ========== PAGE 1: RACE DEI HETEROGENEITY - 2x2 LAYOUT ==========
cat("\\subsection*{Heterogeneity by Race DEI View}\n\n")
cat("Reference group: White Male. Respondent fixed effects are NOT included to allow identification of all DEI interaction terms. Respondent characteristics (age, age$^2$, age$^3$, gender, race, education, income) are included as controls.\n\n")

cat("\\begin{table}[H]\n")
cat("\\centering\n")
cat("\\begin{minipage}[t]{0.48\\textwidth}\n")
cat("\\centering\n")
cat("\\textbf{HR - Binary Race DEI}\n\n")
modelsummary(
  models_hr_dei_race_binary,
  stars = c('*' = 0.1, '**' = 0.05, '***' = 0.01),
  coef_map = coef_map_race_binary,
  gof_map = c("nobs", "r.squared"),
  output = "kableExtra",
  table.attr = "style='width:100%;'"
) %>%
  kable_styling(font_size = 7, latex_options = "HOLD_position")
cat("\\end{minipage}\n")
cat("\\hfill\n")
cat("\\begin{minipage}[t]{0.48\\textwidth}\n")
cat("\\centering\n")
cat("\\textbf{Engineer - Binary Race DEI}\n\n")
modelsummary(
  models_eng_dei_race_binary,
  stars = c('*' = 0.1, '**' = 0.05, '***' = 0.01),
  coef_map = coef_map_race_binary,
  gof_map = c("nobs", "r.squared"),
  output = "kableExtra",
  table.attr = "style='width:100%;'"
) %>%
  kable_styling(font_size = 7, latex_options = "HOLD_position")
cat("\\end{minipage}\n")
cat("\\end{table}\n\n")

cat("\\vspace{0.5cm}\n\n")

cat("\\begin{table}[H]\n")
cat("\\centering\n")
cat("\\begin{minipage}[t]{0.48\\textwidth}\n")
cat("\\centering\n")
cat("\\textbf{HR - Continuous Race DEI}\n\n")
modelsummary(
  models_hr_dei_race_continuous,
  stars = c('*' = 0.1, '**' = 0.05, '***' = 0.01),
  coef_map = coef_map_race_continuous,
  gof_map = c("nobs", "r.squared"),
  output = "kableExtra",
  table.attr = "style='width:100%;'"
) %>%
  kable_styling(font_size = 7, latex_options = "HOLD_position")
cat("\\end{minipage}\n")
cat("\\hfill\n")
cat("\\begin{minipage}[t]{0.48\\textwidth}\n")
cat("\\centering\n")
cat("\\textbf{Engineer - Continuous Race DEI}\n\n")
modelsummary(
  models_eng_dei_race_continuous,
  stars = c('*' = 0.1, '**' = 0.05, '***' = 0.01),
  coef_map = coef_map_race_continuous,
  gof_map = c("nobs", "r.squared"),
  output = "kableExtra",
  table.attr = "style='width:100%;'"
) %>%
  kable_styling(font_size = 7, latex_options = "HOLD_position")
cat("\\end{minipage}\n")
cat("\\end{table}\n\n")

cat("\\newpage\n")

## ========== PAGE 2: GENDER DEI HETEROGENEITY - 2x2 LAYOUT ==========
cat("\\subsection*{Heterogeneity by Gender DEI View}\n\n")
cat("Reference group: White Male. Respondent fixed effects are NOT included to allow identification of all DEI interaction terms. Respondent characteristics (age, age$^2$, age$^3$, gender, race, education, income) are included as controls.\n\n")

cat("\\begin{table}[H]\n")
cat("\\centering\n")
cat("\\begin{minipage}[t]{0.48\\textwidth}\n")
cat("\\centering\n")
cat("\\textbf{HR - Binary Gender DEI}\n\n")
modelsummary(
  models_hr_dei_gender_binary,
  stars = c('*' = 0.1, '**' = 0.05, '***' = 0.01),
  coef_map = coef_map_gender_binary,
  gof_map = c("nobs", "r.squared"),
  output = "kableExtra",
  table.attr = "style='width:100%;'"
) %>%
  kable_styling(font_size = 7, latex_options = "HOLD_position")
cat("\\end{minipage}\n")
cat("\\hfill\n")
cat("\\begin{minipage}[t]{0.48\\textwidth}\n")
cat("\\centering\n")
cat("\\textbf{Engineer - Binary Gender DEI}\n\n")
modelsummary(
  models_eng_dei_gender_binary,
  stars = c('*' = 0.1, '**' = 0.05, '***' = 0.01),
  coef_map = coef_map_gender_binary,
  gof_map = c("nobs", "r.squared"),
  output = "kableExtra",
  table.attr = "style='width:100%;'"
) %>%
  kable_styling(font_size = 7, latex_options = "HOLD_position")
cat("\\end{minipage}\n")
cat("\\end{table}\n\n")

cat("\\vspace{0.5cm}\n\n")

cat("\\begin{table}[H]\n")
cat("\\centering\n")
cat("\\begin{minipage}[t]{0.48\\textwidth}\n")
cat("\\centering\n")
cat("\\textbf{HR - Continuous Gender DEI}\n\n")
modelsummary(
  models_hr_dei_gender_continuous,
  stars = c('*' = 0.1, '**' = 0.05, '***' = 0.01),
  coef_map = coef_map_gender_continuous,
  gof_map = c("nobs", "r.squared"),
  output = "kableExtra",
  table.attr = "style='width:100%;'"
) %>%
  kable_styling(font_size = 7, latex_options = "HOLD_position")
cat("\\end{minipage}\n")
cat("\\hfill\n")
cat("\\begin{minipage}[t]{0.48\\textwidth}\n")
cat("\\centering\n")
cat("\\textbf{Engineer - Continuous Gender DEI}\n\n")
modelsummary(
  models_eng_dei_gender_continuous,
  stars = c('*' = 0.1, '**' = 0.05, '***' = 0.01),
  coef_map = coef_map_gender_continuous,
  gof_map = c("nobs", "r.squared"),
  output = "kableExtra",
  table.attr = "style='width:100%;'"
) %>%
  kable_styling(font_size = 7, latex_options = "HOLD_position")
cat("\\end{minipage}\n")
cat("\\end{table}\n\n")

## Clean up
rm(models_hr_dei_race_binary, models_eng_dei_race_binary,
   models_hr_dei_gender_binary, models_eng_dei_gender_binary,
   models_hr_dei_race_continuous, models_eng_dei_race_continuous,
   models_hr_dei_gender_continuous, models_eng_dei_gender_continuous,
   coef_map_race_binary, coef_map_race_continuous,
   coef_map_gender_binary, coef_map_gender_continuous)
```

\clearpage
## By DEI (New)

The original "By DEI" section above interacts an under-represented-minority indicator with a four-item DEI index (`pro_dei_index_z`). The new analysis below makes two refinements suggested by the survey-item structure: (i) **match** the heterogeneity proxy to the demographic dimension being tested — race-DEI view (`pro_dei_race_z`) for race premia, gender-DEI view (`pro_dei_gender_z`) for gender premia; and (ii) **drop** `s5_view_hiring_dei` from any composite, since it is an empirical belief about the *effect* of DEI on hiring quality and is therefore prone to mechanical correlation with the demographic-evaluation behavior we are trying to measure. We also report two composite indices built only from the normative items — one with and one without the personal diversity preference (`s5_view_diverse`) — and `s5_view_diverse` standalone for completeness. All specifications use White Male as the reference applicant and are restricted to the non-blind arm. Composite indices are constructed inline as the row-mean of the named components, then re-standardized within the analysis sample.

```{r dei_new_setup, eval = TRUE, echo = FALSE, include = FALSE}
## Working data: non-blind only, by role
dt_hr  <- copy(firm_long_dt[treat == "nonblind" & role == "HR"])
dt_eng <- copy(firm_long_dt[treat == "nonblind" & role == "Eng"])

## Build composite indices, restandardized to within-sample SD = 1
zstd <- function(x) (x - mean(x, na.rm = TRUE)) / sd(x, na.rm = TRUE)
build_idx <- function(d, comps) zstd(rowMeans(as.matrix(d[, ..comps]), na.rm = FALSE))

dt_hr [, pro_dei_norm_z := build_idx(.SD, c("pro_dei_race_z", "pro_dei_gender_z"))]
dt_eng[, pro_dei_norm_z := build_idx(.SD, c("pro_dei_race_z", "pro_dei_gender_z"))]
dt_hr [, pro_dei_full_z := build_idx(.SD, c("pro_dei_race_z", "pro_dei_gender_z", "pro_diverse_z"))]
dt_eng[, pro_dei_full_z := build_idx(.SD, c("pro_dei_race_z", "pro_dei_gender_z", "pro_diverse_z"))]

## Helper: fit four feols models for a given continuous proxy.
## `spec = "race_gender"` uses the 6-way demographic cut (White_Male ref);
## `spec = "urm_tech"`   collapses to URM (WF,AF,HM,HF) vs Majority (WM,AM).
fit_hetro <- function(proxy, spec = c("race_gender", "urm_tech")) {
  spec <- match.arg(spec)
  fmla <- if (spec == "race_gender") {
    function(y) as.formula(sprintf(
      "%s ~ i(race_gender, ref='White_Male') + i(race_gender, %s, ref='White_Male') | responseid + resume_index + r_do",
      y, proxy))
  } else {
    function(y) as.formula(sprintf(
      "%s ~ urm_tech + urm_tech:%s | responseid + resume_index + r_do",
      y, proxy))
  }
  list(
    "HR: Coding"   = feols(fmla("sc_coding_z"),  data = dt_hr,  vcov = ~ responseid),
    "HR: Overall"  = feols(fmla("sc_overall_z"), data = dt_hr,  vcov = ~ responseid),
    "Eng: Coding"  = feols(fmla("sc_coding_z"),  data = dt_eng, vcov = ~ responseid),
    "Eng: Overall" = feols(fmla("sc_overall_z"), data = dt_eng, vcov = ~ responseid)
  )
}

chk <- "\\checkmark"

format_tbl <- function(tbl) {
  tbl <- gsub("\\\\par \\\\raggedright", "\\\\par \\\\centering \\\\footnotesize", tbl)
  # Inject Panel A: HR | Panel B: Engineer header + Coding/Overall labels per column
  tbl <- sub(
    "\\\\toprule",
    paste0("\\\\toprule",
           " & \\\\multicolumn{2}{c}{\\\\textbf{Panel A: HR}}",
           " & \\\\multicolumn{2}{c}{\\\\textbf{Panel B: Engineer}} \\\\\\\\ ",
           "\\\\cmidrule(lr){2-3} \\\\cmidrule(lr){4-5} ",
           " & Coding & Overall & Coding & Overall \\\\\\\\"),
    tbl
  )
  # Drop the default duplicate dep-var header row (sc_coding_z, sc_overall_z, ...)
  tbl <- sub(
    "\\s*&\\s*sc_coding_z\\s*&\\s*sc_overall_z\\s*&\\s*sc_coding_z\\s*&\\s*sc_overall_z\\s*\\\\\\\\",
    "",
    tbl
  )
  tbl <- gsub("\\\\begin\\{table\\}", "\\\\begin{table}\n\\\\resizebox{\\\\textwidth}{!}{", tbl)
  tbl <- gsub("\\\\end\\{table\\}", "}\n\\\\end{table}", tbl)
  tbl
}

label_dict <- function(proxy, proxy_label, spec = c("race_gender", "urm_tech")) {
  spec <- match.arg(spec)
  if (spec == "race_gender") {
    groups <- c("White_Female"   = "White Female",
                "Asian_Male"     = "Asian Male",
                "Asian_Female"   = "Asian Female",
                "Hispanic_Male"  = "Hispanic Male",
                "Hispanic_Female"= "Hispanic Female")
    c(setNames(groups, paste0("race_gender::", names(groups))),
      setNames(paste0(groups, " $\\times$ ", proxy_label),
               paste0("race_gender::", names(groups), ":", proxy)))
  } else {
    c("urm_tech"                          = "URM (WF, AF, HM, HF)",
      setNames(paste0("URM $\\times$ ", proxy_label), paste0("urm_tech:", proxy)))
  }
}

print_etable <- function(models, proxy, proxy_label, title, notes, spec = c("race_gender", "urm_tech")) {
  spec <- match.arg(spec)
  tbl <- etable(
    models,
    tex = TRUE, style.tex = style.tex("aer"),
    placement = "H",
    depvar = FALSE,
    keep = if (spec == "race_gender") "%race_gender" else "%urm_tech",
    fitstat = c("n", "ar2"),
    drop.section = "fixef",
    digits = 3, se.below = TRUE,
    signif.code = c("***" = 0.01, "**" = 0.05, "*" = 0.1),
    dict = label_dict(proxy, proxy_label, spec),
    extralines = list(
      "Resume FE"     = rep(chk, 4),
      "Respondent FE" = rep(chk, 4),
      "Position FE"   = rep(chk, 4)
    ),
    title = title,
    notes = notes
  )
  cat(format_tbl(tbl))
}

## Wrapper: print BOTH cuts (6-demographic race_gender, then URM-binary) for one proxy.
print_both <- function(proxy, proxy_label, notes_base) {
  print_etable(
    fit_hetro(proxy, spec = "race_gender"),
    proxy = proxy, proxy_label = proxy_label, spec = "race_gender",
    title = sprintf("Heterogeneity by %s — 6-Group Demographic Cut", proxy_label),
    notes = notes_base
  )
  cat("\n\\vspace{0.5em}\n\n")
  print_etable(
    fit_hetro(proxy, spec = "urm_tech"),
    proxy = proxy, proxy_label = proxy_label, spec = "urm_tech",
    title = sprintf("Heterogeneity by %s — URM-Tech Binary Cut", proxy_label),
    notes = notes_base
  )
}

## ------- Triple interaction helpers (race_gender x proxy_a x proxy_b) -------
fit_triple <- function(proxy_a, proxy_b, spec = c("race_gender", "urm_tech")) {
  spec <- match.arg(spec)
  fmla <- if (spec == "race_gender") {
    function(y) as.formula(sprintf(
      "%s ~ i(race_gender, ref='White_Male') * %s * %s | responseid + resume_index + r_do",
      y, proxy_a, proxy_b))
  } else {
    function(y) as.formula(sprintf(
      "%s ~ urm_tech * %s * %s | responseid + resume_index + r_do",
      y, proxy_a, proxy_b))
  }
  list(
    "HR: Coding"   = feols(fmla("sc_coding_z"),  data = dt_hr,  vcov = ~ responseid),
    "HR: Overall"  = feols(fmla("sc_overall_z"), data = dt_hr,  vcov = ~ responseid),
    "Eng: Coding"  = feols(fmla("sc_coding_z"),  data = dt_eng, vcov = ~ responseid),
    "Eng: Overall" = feols(fmla("sc_overall_z"), data = dt_eng, vcov = ~ responseid)
  )
}

label_dict_triple <- function(proxy_a, proxy_b, a_lab, b_lab, spec) {
  if (spec == "race_gender") {
    g <- c("White_Female"="WF", "Asian_Male"="AM", "Asian_Female"="AF",
           "Hispanic_Male"="HM", "Hispanic_Female"="HF")
    c(setNames(g, paste0("race_gender::", names(g))),
      setNames(paste0(g, " $\\times$ ", a_lab),
               paste0("race_gender::", names(g), ":", proxy_a)),
      setNames(paste0(g, " $\\times$ ", b_lab),
               paste0("race_gender::", names(g), ":", proxy_b)),
      setNames(paste0(g, " $\\times$ ", a_lab, " $\\times$ ", b_lab),
               paste0("race_gender::", names(g), ":", proxy_a, ":", proxy_b)))
  } else {
    c("urm_tech" = "URM",
      setNames(paste0("URM $\\times$ ", a_lab),              paste0("urm_tech:", proxy_a)),
      setNames(paste0("URM $\\times$ ", b_lab),              paste0("urm_tech:", proxy_b)),
      setNames(paste0("URM $\\times$ ", a_lab, " $\\times$ ", b_lab),
               paste0("urm_tech:", proxy_a, ":", proxy_b)))
  }
}

print_etable_triple <- function(models, proxy_a, proxy_b, a_lab, b_lab, title, notes, spec) {
  tbl <- etable(
    models,
    tex = TRUE, style.tex = style.tex("aer"),
    placement = "H",
    depvar = FALSE,
    keep = if (spec == "race_gender") "%race_gender" else "%urm_tech",
    fitstat = c("n", "ar2"),
    drop.section = "fixef",
    digits = 3, se.below = TRUE,
    signif.code = c("***" = 0.01, "**" = 0.05, "*" = 0.1),
    dict = label_dict_triple(proxy_a, proxy_b, a_lab, b_lab, spec),
    extralines = list(
      "Resume FE"     = rep(chk, 4),
      "Respondent FE" = rep(chk, 4),
      "Position FE"   = rep(chk, 4)
    ),
    title = title,
    notes = notes
  )
  cat(format_tbl(tbl))
}

print_both_triple <- function(proxy_a, proxy_b, a_lab, b_lab, notes_base) {
  print_etable_triple(
    fit_triple(proxy_a, proxy_b, "race_gender"),
    proxy_a, proxy_b, a_lab, b_lab,
    title = sprintf("%s $\\times$ %s $\\times$ Demographic — 6-Group Cut", a_lab, b_lab),
    notes = notes_base, spec = "race_gender"
  )
  cat("\n\\vspace{0.5em}\n\n")
  print_etable_triple(
    fit_triple(proxy_a, proxy_b, "urm_tech"),
    proxy_a, proxy_b, a_lab, b_lab,
    title = sprintf("%s $\\times$ %s $\\times$ URM — Binary Cut", a_lab, b_lab),
    notes = notes_base, spec = "urm_tech"
  )
}
```

\newpage

### Domain-Matched: Race-DEI View

Heterogeneity proxy: z-score of `s5_view_dei_race` (higher = more pro race-DEI). Each group's main-effect row shows the premium over White Male at the proxy mean; the interaction row shows how that premium shifts per 1 SD increase in pro race-DEI view.

```{r dei_new_race, eval = TRUE, echo = FALSE, results = 'asis'}
print_etable(
  fit_hetro("pro_dei_race_z"),
  proxy = "pro_dei_race_z",
  proxy_label = "Pro Race-DEI",
  title = "Heterogeneity by Race-DEI View (Ref: White Male, Non-blind)",
  notes = "Standard errors clustered by respondent."
)
```

\newpage

### Domain-Matched: Gender-DEI View

Heterogeneity proxy: z-score of `s5_view_dei_gender` (higher = more pro gender-DEI).

```{r dei_new_gender, eval = TRUE, echo = FALSE, results = 'asis'}
print_etable(
  fit_hetro("pro_dei_gender_z"),
  proxy = "pro_dei_gender_z",
  proxy_label = "Pro Gender-DEI",
  title = "Heterogeneity by Gender-DEI View (Ref: White Male, Non-blind)",
  notes = "Standard errors clustered by respondent."
)
```

\newpage

### Combined Normative Index (Race-DEI + Gender-DEI)

Heterogeneity proxy: row-mean of `pro_dei_race_z` and `pro_dei_gender_z`, re-standardized within sample. Excludes both `pro_diverse_z` and `pro_hiring_dei_z`.

```{r dei_new_combined, eval = TRUE, echo = FALSE, results = 'asis'}
print_etable(
  fit_hetro("pro_dei_norm_z"),
  proxy = "pro_dei_norm_z",
  proxy_label = "Pro DEI (Race+Gender)",
  title = "Heterogeneity by Composite Race+Gender DEI Index (Ref: White Male, Non-blind)",
  notes = "Standard errors clustered by respondent."
)
```

\newpage

### Combined Index Including Diversity Preference

Heterogeneity proxy: row-mean of `pro_dei_race_z`, `pro_dei_gender_z`, and `pro_diverse_z`, re-standardized within sample. Adds the personal diversity-workplace preference to the normative index.

```{r dei_new_combined_diverse, eval = TRUE, echo = FALSE, results = 'asis'}
print_etable(
  fit_hetro("pro_dei_full_z"),
  proxy = "pro_dei_full_z",
  proxy_label = "Pro DEI (R+G+Div)",
  title = "Heterogeneity by Composite Race+Gender+Diverse Index (Ref: White Male, Non-blind)",
  notes = "Standard errors clustered by respondent."
)
```

\newpage

### Standalone: Diversity Preference

Heterogeneity proxy: z-score of `s5_view_diverse` alone (higher = stronger personal preference for working in a diverse environment).

```{r dei_new_diverse_only, eval = TRUE, echo = FALSE, results = 'asis'}
print_etable(
  fit_hetro("pro_diverse_z"),
  proxy = "pro_diverse_z",
  proxy_label = "Pro Diverse",
  title = "Heterogeneity by Diversity Preference Standalone (Ref: White Male, Non-blind)",
  notes = "Standard errors clustered by respondent."
)
```

```{r dei_new_cleanup, eval = TRUE, echo = FALSE, include = FALSE}
## Intentionally NOT removing helpers (fit_hetro, print_etable, etc.) here —
## the following "By DEI (Alternative Proxies)" section reuses them.
rm(zstd, build_idx)
```

\clearpage
## By DEI (Alternative Proxies)

The proxies below swap in revealed-behavior or firm-level measures for the stated normative items used above. Same model, same sample (non-blind), same reference applicant (White Male).

```{r dei_alt_setup, eval = TRUE, echo = FALSE, include = FALSE}
## Reuse helpers defined in the "By DEI (New)" setup chunk, but rebuild the
## per-role data.tables since dt_hr / dt_eng were also reused from there.
```

\newpage
### Priority of "Diversity and Representation" in Hiring Goals (`s4_goals_5`)

Revealed priority from the Section 4 forced-choice ranking of 7 hiring goals (asked *before* the DEI attitude block). Proxy: z-score of the inverted rank (higher = diversity ranked more highly relative to six competing goals including Technical Ability, Communication, etc.).

```{r dei_alt_div_priority, eval = TRUE, echo = FALSE, results = 'asis'}
print_both(
  "div_priority_z", "Div Priority",
  "Proxy: z-score of (8 - s4_goals_5), where s4_goals_5 is the rank (1=top, 7=bottom) assigned to \"Diversity and Representation.\" Sample: non-blind."
)
```

\newpage
### Firm DEI Policy Count (`s5_policy_dei`)

Count of firm DEI policies (0–6) the respondent reports their firm has, from the checklist of six concrete policies (non-discrimination, URM representation, DEI training, DEI staff, salary transparency, affinity groups). Firm-behavioral: firm had to actually adopt each. Proxy: z-scored count.

```{r dei_alt_policy_count, eval = TRUE, echo = FALSE, results = 'asis'}
print_both(
  "dei_policy_count_z", "Policy Count",
  "Proxy: z-score of the count of six dei_policy_* indicators (s5_policy_dei). Sample: non-blind."
)
```

\newpage
### Firm Uses Blind Hiring (`s5_bh`)

Binary indicator: firm blinds any information at any stage of hiring. Narrowly on-topic (firm has itself adopted the practice being studied). Proxy: `bh_yes` (0/1).

```{r dei_alt_bh, eval = TRUE, echo = FALSE, results = 'asis'}
print_both(
  "bh_yes", "Firm Blinds",
  "Proxy: bh_yes = 1 if firm blinds any info at any stage of hiring. Sample: non-blind."
)
```

\newpage
### Firm Targets URM/Female Groups (`s5_policy_diversity`)

Binary indicator: firm is actively interested in advancing Black, Hispanic, or Female representation in technical roles (multi-select; any of the three counts). Firm-behavioral intent. Proxy: `firm_targets_any_urm` (0/1).

```{r dei_alt_targets, eval = TRUE, echo = FALSE, results = 'asis'}
print_both(
  "firm_targets_any_urm", "Firm Targets URM",
  "Proxy: 1 if firm targets any of {Black, Hispanic, Female} for representation (s5_policy_diversity). Sample: non-blind."
)
```

\newpage
### Firm Attention to Race DEI (`s5_view_dei_race_f`)

Respondent's perception of how much their firm currently pays attention to increasing racial DEI (1=Very low … 5=Very high). Middle-ground: perceived firm behavior, not personal view on whether DEI is good. Proxy: z-score.

```{r dei_alt_attention_race, eval = TRUE, echo = FALSE, results = 'asis'}
print_both(
  "firm_attention_race_z", "Firm Race-DEI Attention",
  "Proxy: z-score of s5_view_dei_race_f (respondent's perception of firm's current race-DEI effort). Sample: non-blind."
)
```

\newpage
### Firm Attention to Gender DEI (`s5_view_dei_gender_f`)

Same construction as above but for gender DEI attention. Proxy: z-score of `s5_view_dei_gender_f`.

```{r dei_alt_attention_gender, eval = TRUE, echo = FALSE, results = 'asis'}
print_both(
  "firm_attention_gender_z", "Firm Gender-DEI Attention",
  "Proxy: z-score of s5_view_dei_gender_f (respondent's perception of firm's current gender-DEI effort). Sample: non-blind."
)
```

\newpage
### Triple Interaction: Personal Priority $\times$ Firm Attention (Backlash Test)

The race-attention single interaction above showed a negative coefficient on Hispanic Male $\times$ firm race-DEI attention (HR Overall $\approx -0.27^{***}$). One reading is **backlash**: respondents at firms with high perceived race-DEI attention — but whose *personal* diversity priority is low — feel the firm is over-weighting DEI and downgrade URM candidates in response. That hypothesis predicts a *positive* triple interaction: higher personal priority flips the firm-attention effect from negative to positive. Below we test this with `div_priority_z` $\times$ firm-attention, separately for race and gender attention.

```{r dei_alt_triple_race, eval = TRUE, echo = FALSE, results = 'asis', warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
print_both_triple(
  "div_priority_z", "firm_attention_race_z",
  "Div Prio", "Race Attn",
  "div_priority_z = z-score of personal diversity priority (s4_goals_5); firm_attention_race_z = z-score of perceived firm race-DEI attention (s5_view_dei_race_f). Sample: non-blind."
)
```

\newpage
```{r dei_alt_triple_gender, eval = TRUE, echo = FALSE, results = 'asis', warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
print_both_triple(
  "div_priority_z", "firm_attention_gender_z",
  "Div Prio", "Gender Attn",
  "div_priority_z = z-score of personal diversity priority (s4_goals_5); firm_attention_gender_z = z-score of perceived firm gender-DEI attention (s5_view_dei_gender_f). Sample: non-blind."
)
```

```{r dei_alt_cleanup, eval = TRUE, echo = FALSE, include = FALSE}
rm(dt_hr, dt_eng, fit_hetro, fit_triple, chk, format_tbl,
   label_dict, label_dict_triple, print_etable, print_etable_triple,
   print_both, print_both_triple)
```

\clearpage
## By DEI (Storyline)

### What we tested

A natural story would be: respondents with stronger pro-DEI views should exhibit larger premia toward under-represented applicants. We test that across **11 heterogeneity proxies** — five stated preferences (the four DEI-view items plus their composites), four firm-level behaviors (policy count, blind-hiring use, URM targeting, perceived attention), and one forced-choice revealed priority (`s4_goals` rank of Diversity) — interacted with applicant demographics two ways (6-group and URM-binary).

### What we found (one line)

**Stated and single revealed-preference proxies do not meaningfully shift demographic premia.** Across 132 interaction tests (11 proxies × 4 outcomes × 3 interaction terms on average), significance counts are close to chance. The URM-binary cut is especially striking: **0 of 44 interactions reach p < 0.10**. No single DEI proxy — by itself — explains who gives URM candidates a premium.

### The one signal, and what it looks like

Exception: the **triple interaction** of personal diversity priority with firm-perceived DEI attention is consistently negative for HR (p < 0.05 on both coding and overall outcomes, for both race- and gender-attention). Reading the HR-Overall regression for Hispanic Male candidates at $\pm 1$ SD of each covariate gives:

```{r dei_story_table, eval = TRUE, echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE, results = 'asis'}
dt_hr_nb <- firm_long_dt[treat == "nonblind" & role == "HR"]
m <- feols(
  sc_overall_z ~ i(race_gender, ref = "White_Male") * div_priority_z * firm_attention_race_z
  | responseid + resume_index + r_do,
  data = dt_hr_nb, vcov = ~ responseid
)

b <- coef(m)
b_hm     <- b["race_gender::Hispanic_Male"]
b_hm_div <- b["div_priority_z:race_gender::Hispanic_Male"]
b_hm_atn <- b["firm_attention_race_z:race_gender::Hispanic_Male"]
b_hm_tri <- b["div_priority_z:firm_attention_race_z:race_gender::Hispanic_Male"]

q <- function(div, atn) b_hm + b_hm_div*div + b_hm_atn*atn + b_hm_tri*div*atn

tab <- data.frame(
  Personal_Priority = c("Low (personal, $-1\\sigma$)", "High (personal, $+1\\sigma$)"),
  Low_Firm   = sprintf("%+.2f", c(q(-1,-1), q(+1,-1))),
  High_Firm  = sprintf("%+.2f", c(q(-1,+1), q(+1,+1)))
)

knitr::kable(
  tab, format = "latex", booktabs = TRUE, escape = FALSE,
  col.names = c(" ", "Low Firm Race Attn", "High Firm Race Attn"),
  caption = "Predicted Hispanic Male premium (vs. White Male) on HR Overall rating, by quadrant of respondent's personal Diversity priority (s4\\_goals\\_5) and perceived firm race-DEI attention (s5\\_view\\_dei\\_race\\_f). Non-blind arm, $N=1{,}188$."
) |>
  kableExtra::kable_styling(latex_options = "HOLD_position") |>
  kableExtra::add_header_above(c(" " = 1, "Firm Race-DEI Attention" = 2))

rm(dt_hr_nb, m, b, b_hm, b_hm_div, b_hm_atn, b_hm_tri, q, tab)
```

### Interpretation: substitution, not backlash

A "backlash" reading of the single interaction on firm race-attention (negative coefficient on HM $\times$ firm attention) would predict a **positive** triple interaction: anti-DEI respondents at pro-DEI firms should be the ones penalizing URM candidates. The data does the opposite.

What the quadrant table actually shows:

1. The **largest HM premium** ($+0.74\sigma$) appears in HR respondents who **personally prioritize diversity** *and* work at firms that **don't**. These are compensators — filling a gap they see their firm as leaving open.
2. Once the firm **is** emphasizing race-DEI, personal priority stops mattering. HR respondents at high-attention firms give roughly no HM premium regardless of their own views.
3. The backlash cell — anti-DEI personal, pro-DEI firm — is not a large negative. It is modestly negative, not meaningfully different from the other high-firm-attention cell.

The pattern fits a **substitution** mechanism: personal DEI effort and institutional DEI effort appear to be substitutes in the HR evaluator's behavior, not complements. The "do the work when the firm isn't, and ease off when the firm is" description is consistent with the Hispanic Female triple (similar signs for HR Coding and Overall) and with the parallel result when firm *gender*-attention is swapped in for race-attention.

### Caveats

- The result is **HR-specific**: engineers' triple interactions are null across the board.
- The triple interaction involves two simultaneously-scaled proxies; the quadrant numbers are linear-projection extrapolations, not sample averages. The corner cells contain relatively thin subsamples.
- This is one sample, and with 11 primary proxies tested before we arrived at the triple, the "storyline" bears some selection. It would be strongest as a *replication target* rather than a confirmed result.

### Implications for the paper

If we choose to report a DEI-heterogeneity story, the defensible framing is:

> *"Neither stated DEI attitudes nor observable firm DEI behaviors, taken singly, explain variation in demographic premia. However, the combination of strong personal diversity priority and a firm that does not visibly act on it is associated with a substantially larger Hispanic-Male premium among HR evaluators — a pattern consistent with individual-level substitution for perceived firm inattention, rather than backlash against firm over-emphasis."*

This is a cleaner claim than "pro-DEI → more URM premium," which the data doesn't support.

\clearpage
### Non-linear Demographic Biases for Low vs High Quality Candidates (GPA & Coding Score)

```{r nonlinear_preferences, echo = FALSE, include = FALSE}

# ==============================================================================
# HELPER FUNCTIONS
# ==============================================================================
extract_split_data <- function(model_split, role_label, tier_labels) {
  plot_list <- list()
  for (i in seq_along(model_split)) {
    df <- as.data.frame(coeftable(model_split[[i]]))
    df$Term <- rownames(df)
    df$Tier <- tier_labels[i]
    plot_list[[i]] <- df
  }
  plot_df <- do.call(rbind, plot_list)
  plot_df$Term <- gsub("race_gender::", "", plot_df$Term)
  plot_df$Term <- gsub("_", " ", plot_df$Term)
  plot_df$Term <- factor(plot_df$Term, levels = unique(plot_df$Term))
  plot_df$stars <- ifelse(plot_df$`Pr(>|t|)` < 0.01, "***",
                   ifelse(plot_df$`Pr(>|t|)` < 0.05, "**",
                   ifelse(plot_df$`Pr(>|t|)` < 0.1, "*", "")))
  plot_df$Role <- role_label
  plot_df$Tier <- factor(plot_df$Tier, levels = tier_labels)
  return(plot_df)
}

make_split_plot <- function(plot_df, title_label, color_vals, shape_vals, show_y = TRUE) {
  p <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = Term, y = Estimate, color = Tier, shape = Tier)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "black", linewidth = 0.5) +
    geom_pointrange(aes(ymin = Estimate - 1.96 * `Std. Error`,
                        ymax = Estimate + 1.96 * `Std. Error`),
                    position = position_dodge(width = 0.6), size = 0.4) +
    geom_text(aes(label = stars, y = Estimate + 1.96 * `Std. Error` + 0.02),
              position = position_dodge(width = 0.6), size = 3.5, vjust = 0, 
              color = "black", show.legend = FALSE) +
    scale_color_manual(values = color_vals) +
    scale_shape_manual(values = shape_vals) +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.1, 0.15))) +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-0.65, 0.65)) +
    labs(title = title_label,
         x = "Applicant Demographic (vs. White Male)",
         y = "Predicted Premium (Overall Z-Score)",
         color = "Tier:", shape = "Tier:") +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 8),
          axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),
          axis.title = element_text(size = 9),
          legend.position = "top",
          legend.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 8),
          legend.text = element_text(size = 8),
          plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 9),
          plot.margin = margin(5, 10, 5, 5, "pt"))
  if (!show_y) p <- p + theme(axis.title.y = element_blank())
  return(p)
}
```


#### HR

```{r nonlinear_pref_HR, eval = TRUE, echo = FALSE, fig.width=9, fig.height=6, fig.align='center'}

# ==============================================================================
# (GPA) High / Mid / Low - Six Demographics
# ==============================================================================
hr_gpa_split <- feols(sc_overall_z ~ i(race_gender, ref="White_Male") | 
                      resume_index + responseid + r_do, 
                      split = ~gpa_tier3, cluster = ~responseid,
                      data = firm_long_dt[treat == "nonblind" & role == "HR" & s3_outside_res == 0])

gpa_tier3s <- c("Low GPA", "Mid GPA", "Top GPA")
gpa_colors <- c("Low GPA" = "black", "Mid GPA" = "steelblue", "Top GPA" = "indianred1")
gpa_shapes <- c("Low GPA" = 16, "Mid GPA" = 15, "Top GPA" = 17)

hr_gpa_df <- extract_split_data(hr_gpa_split, "HR", gpa_tier3s)

# gpa_tier2s <- c("Non_Elite_GPA", "Elite_GPA")
# gpa_colors <- c("Non_Elite_GPA" = "black", "Elite_GPA" = "steelblue")
# gpa_shapes <- c("Non_Elite_GPA" = 16, "Elite_GPA" = 15)
# 
# hr_gpa_df <- extract_split_data(hr_gpa_split, "HR", gpa_tier2s)

p_hr_gpa <- make_split_plot(hr_gpa_df, "HR: Premium by GPA Tier", gpa_colors, gpa_shapes, show_y = TRUE)

# ==============================================================================
# (GPA) High / Mid / Low - URM
# ==============================================================================

hr_urm_gpa_split <- feols(sc_overall_z ~ urm_tech | 
                          resume_index + responseid + r_do, 
                          split = ~gpa_tier3, cluster = ~responseid,
                          data = firm_long_dt[treat == "nonblind" & role == "HR" & s3_outside_res == 0])

hr_urm_gpa_df   <- extract_split_data(hr_urm_gpa_split,  "HR",       gpa_tier3s)

p_hr_urm_gpa   <- make_split_plot(hr_urm_gpa_df,  "HR: URM Premium by GPA Tier",   gpa_colors,  gpa_shapes,  show_y = TRUE)

# ==============================================================================
# Coding Ability (Top vs Bottom Coding Score or Test Cases) - Six Demographics
# ==============================================================================
hr_test_split <- feols(sc_overall_z ~ i(race_gender, ref="White_Male") | 
                       resume_index + responseid + r_do, 
                       split = ~top_half_coder, cluster = ~responseid,
                       data = firm_long_dt[treat == "nonblind" & role == "HR" & s3_outside_res == 0])

test_tiers <- c("Bottom 50% Coders", "Top 50% Coders")
test_colors <- c("Bottom 50% Coders" = "black", "Top 50% Coders" = "indianred1")
test_shapes <- c("Bottom 50% Coders" = 16, "Top 50% Coders" = 17)

hr_test_df <- extract_split_data(hr_test_split, "HR", test_tiers)

p_hr_test <- make_split_plot(hr_test_df, "HR: Premium by Coding Ability", test_colors, test_shapes, show_y = TRUE)

# ==============================================================================
```
